_comment: Illustrative few-shot. Input is the assembled context fragment; output is the structured SOAP. Used to anchor format, not to memorize content.
input:
[clip 1, 00:00]
Patient: I've had this cough for about three weeks now. It's worse at night. No fever but I do feel a bit short of breath when I climb the stairs.
Clinician: Any chest pain? Bringing up phlegm?
Patient: No chest pain. A bit of clear phlegm in the mornings.

[clip 2, 00:00]
Clinician: I'm going to listen to your chest. ... Mild wheeze on the right side. Sats 96 on room air. Heart rate 78, regular. BP 124 over 78.

[clip 3, 00:00]
Clinician: I think this is most likely a post-viral cough with some bronchial irritation, but given the three-week duration I'd like to rule out anything else. I'm going to order a chest X-ray and a basic blood panel. Try a salbutamol inhaler — two puffs as needed for the breathlessness. If you get any chest pain, high fever, or coughing up blood, go to A&E. Otherwise see me again in two weeks with the results.

output.subjective: Three-week history of cough, worse at night, with associated mild exertional dyspnoea on stair-climbing [clip 1]. No fever. Small volume clear sputum in the mornings. No chest pain.
output.objective: Mild expiratory wheeze right side. SpO2 96% room air. HR 78 regular. BP 124/78 [clip 2].
output.assessment: Likely post-viral cough with bronchial irritation; duration warrants exclusion of alternative causes [clip 3].
plan: Trial salbutamol inhaler, 2 puffs PRN for breathlessness.
Safety net: present to A&E for chest pain, high fever, or haemoptysis.
